titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to Sir Charles Edward Troup, 8 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Sir Edward O'Farrell (c.1857-1926), the Assistant Under Secretary to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, on behalf of Sir Ivor Churchill Guest, Lord Wimborne (1973-1939), Lord Lieutenant of Ireland.The letter was addressed to Sir Charles Edward Troup (1857-1941). Troup was a civil servant, becoming permanent Under Secretary from 1908 until 1922. He took special interest in police and criminal work while employed in the Home office.In this letter O'Farrell is forwarding an attached application for the release of Gustav Adolf Etter a present prisoner of war in Oldcastle Detention Camp. He is also forwarding a report from the Dublin Metropolitan Police.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3906

correspAction.placeName: Home Office, Whitehall, London S.W., United Kingdom

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
8th February,1916. 2265 Sir, I am directed by the Lord Lieutenant to forward to
you, to be laid before the Secretary of State, the attached
application for release from internment from Gustav Adolf Etter ,
at present a prisoner of war in Oldcastle Detention Camp
with report thereon from the Dublin Metropolitan police. His Excellency desires me to say that he concurs in
the view of the General Officer Commanding the Troops in
Ireland that this man should not be released from detention. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, E O'Farrell The Under Secretary of State, Home Office, Whitehall, London, S.W.
